free_text: Activation cross-sections of long lived products of
deuteron induced nuclear reactions on dysprosium up
to 50 MeV
Natural dysprosium foil, thickness: 100.59 microns
A stack containing
natural Dy foils as target, natural Al foils as
monitor foils and Tb foils as energy degraders and
other target material were irradiated with a deuteron
beam of 50 MeV, 120nA for 30 minutes. Gamma spectra
were measured non-destructively in 4 series using
general cooling times of 4h, 30h, 430h and 2380h.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way following the
recommendations of the ISO-guide as the square root of
the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
is not included.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL-2011,
TENDL-2012, ALICE-IPPE-D and EMPIRE-II-D.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
VS. SF5: M+ deleted in 003 and 010.
SF4: -G added in subentry 004.
